problem: Ben rolls four fair 20-sided dice, and each of the dice has faces numbered from 1 to 20. What is the probability that exactly two of the dice show an even number?

classified_questions.question: Can you explain the concept of probability?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How many sides does each of the 20-sided dice have?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the probability of a 20-sided die showing an even number? And what about showing an odd number?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How many dice does Ben roll?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we calculate the probability of getting exactly two dice showing even numbers?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many ways can we choose which two dice show even numbers out of the four?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the probability of one specific arrangement of two even and two odd rolls happening?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we calculate the overall probability of all six possible arrangements occurring?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Using the answers from the previous questions, can you calculate the probability of exactly two dice showing even numbers?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you think of any similar problems where we can apply the same concepts of probability and counting?
